narrative.description: Wii Music (2008) is a acclaimed rhythm title recognized in retro gaming lists for its distinctive mechanics, pacing, and lasting influence on the genre.
narrative.core_loop: Hit note prompts in time with music across escalating song challenges.

mechanics.map_notes: Core rhythm tap timing loop defines Wii Music's identity.
mechanics.expression: Notes scroll to a beat line; players tap, hold, or slide on precise timing windows.

mechanics.map_notes: Core score chain multiplier loop defines Wii Music's identity.
mechanics.expression: Consecutive successful inputs build a multiplier that boosts final score.

mechanics.map_notes: Core song stage progression loop defines Wii Music's identity.
mechanics.expression: Unlock and play through a curated track list with increasing difficulty tiers.

mechanics.map_notes: Mission Select Structure supports the main loop in Wii Music.
mechanics.expression: A menu or hub lets the player choose missions or levels in flexible order with difficulty ratings.

mechanics.map_notes: Life Based Retry System supports the main loop in Wii Music.
mechanics.expression: A failure-buffer system in which the player is granted a limited number of retries, losing one life upon failure and restarting from a previous checkpoint.

mechanics.map_notes: Combo Trick Scoring supports the main loop in Wii Music.
mechanics.expression: Chained tricks and aerial maneuvers multiply score within a combo timer during skating or stunt gameplay.

mechanics.map_notes: Fill Reward Progression Loop supports the main loop in Wii Music.
mechanics.expression: A progression gate where a fixed collection target must be met before an upgrade or skill point becomes available, tying advancement directly to measurable progress.

mechanics.map_notes: Input Aligned Movement supports the main loop in Wii Music.
mechanics.expression: Movement responds directly and immediately to input direction with minimal inertia, optimized for top-down or free-aim combat spaces.

mechanics.map_notes: Multi Line Scoring Multiplier supports the main loop in Wii Music.
mechanics.expression: Clearing multiple lines simultaneously awards disproportionately higher scores than single-line clears.

mechanics.map_notes: Purchase Gated Upgrade supports the main loop in Wii Music.
mechanics.expression: Players collect currency through gameplay and exchange it at vendors for permanent upgrades, creating economic progression choices.

mechanics.map_notes: Bench Checkpoint supports the main loop in Wii Music.
mechanics.expression: Designated rest points save progress, restore health or resources, and set respawn locations on death.

design_intent.player_fantasy: A rhythm experience centered on Wii Music.
